rules.rule: CancellationToken MUST be the last parameter in every method signature.
CancellationToken MUST default to `default` for backwards compatibility.
CancellationToken MUST NOT be nullable (`CancellationToken?` is forbidden).
The parameter MUST be named `cancellationToken` ‚Äî never `token`, `ct`, or `cancelToken`.
Methods accepting CancellationToken MUST call `cancellationToken.ThrowIfCancellationRequested()` near the start of the method before dependency operations.
The same CancellationToken MUST flow through all layers (Controller ‚Üí Coordination ‚Üí Orchestration ‚Üí Foundation ‚Üí Broker ‚Üí Dependency).
Never silently drop the token when calling dependencies.
When timeout support is required, use `CancellationTokenSource.CreateLinkedTokenSource(cancellationToken, timeoutSource.Token)`.
Do not create a new `CancellationTokenSource` unnecessarily ‚Äî link to the upstream token instead.
When timeout logic exists, BOTH catch blocks MUST be present in the correct order.
The timeout-guarded catch block `catch (OperationCanceledException) when (timeoutSource.IsCancellationRequested)` MUST precede the plain `catch (OperationCanceledException)` block.
TimeoutException MAY be wrapped as a dependency failure (e.g., `DependencyException` or `FailedServiceDependencyException`).
OperationCanceledException MUST NEVER be wrapped in any service or dependency exception ‚Äî it MUST be rethrown with `throw;`.
Whenever a method accepts a CancellationToken, the plain `catch (OperationCanceledException) { throw; }` block MUST exist to prevent it from being caught by the catch-all exception handler.
In parallel orchestration, pass the same CancellationToken to all parallel operations.
CancellationToken SHOULD be used for database operations, HTTP operations, file operations, queues, messaging, and long-running work.
CancellationToken SHOULD NOT be added to trivial in-memory operations (e.g., `public int Add(int x, int y, CancellationToken cancellationToken)`).
Dependency exception tests MUST include `TimeoutException` in Theory MemberData to validate localization and categorization.
Exception tests MUST verify true cancellation (`OperationCanceledException`) is never wrapped ‚Äî it must be rethrown unchanged.
